title: A Dream
content:
혻혻혻혻Was it a dream? We sail占폻, I thought we sail占폻,
혻혻혻혻Martin and I, down a green Alpine stream,
혻혻혻혻Under o占폾rhanging pines; the morning sun,
혻혻혻혻On the wet umbrage of their glossy tops,
혻혻혻혻On the red pinings of their forest floor,
혻혻혻혻Drew a warm scent abroad; behind the pines
혻혻혻혻The mountain skirts, with all their sylvan change
혻혻혻혻Of bright-leaf占폻 chestnuts, and moss占폻 walnut-trees,
혻혻혻혻And the frail scarlet-berried ash, began.
혻혻혻혻Swiss chalets glitter占폻 on the dewy slopes,
혻혻혻혻And from some swarded shelf high up, there came
혻혻혻혻Notes of wild pastoral music: over all
혻혻혻혻Rang占폻, diamond-bright, the eternal wall of snow.
혻혻혻혻Upon the mossy rocks at the stream占퐏 edge.
혻혻혻혻Back占폻 by the pines, a plank-built cottage stood,
혻혻혻혻Bright in the sun; the climbing gourd-plant占퐏 leaves
혻혻혻혻Muffled its walls, and on the stone-strewn roof
혻혻혻혻Lay the warm golden gourds; golden, within,
혻혻혻혻Under the eaves, peer占폻 rows of Indian corn.
혻혻혻혻We shot beneath the cottage with the stream.
혻혻혻혻On the brown rude-carv占폻 balcony two Forms
혻혻혻혻Came forth, Olivia占퐏, Marguerite! and thine.
혻혻혻혻Clad were they both in white, flowers in their breast;
혻혻혻혻Straw hats bedeck占폻 their heads, with ribbons blue
혻혻혻혻Which wav占폻, and on their shoulders fluttering play占폻.
혻혻혻혻They saw us, they conferr占폻; their bosoms heav占폻,
혻혻혻혻And more than mortal impulse fill占폻 their eyes.
혻혻혻혻Their lips mov占폻; their white arms, wav占폻 eagerly,
혻혻혻혻Flash占폻 once, like falling streams:, we rose, we gaz占폻
혻혻혻혻One moment, on the rapid占퐏 top, our boat
혻혻혻혻Hung pois占폻, and then the darting River of Life,
혻혻혻혻Loud thundering, bore us by: swift, swift it foam占폻;
혻혻혻혻Black under cliffs it rac占폻, round headlands shone.
혻혻혻혻Soon the plank占폻 cottage 占퐉id the sun-warm占폻 pines
혻혻혻혻Faded, the moss, the rocks; us burning Plains
혻혻혻혻Bristled with cities, us the Sea receiv占폻
meaning:
This evocative piece by Matthew Arnold represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Matthew Arnold's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
